name: sca_heavy_payload
description: SCA heavy payload burst — simulates agents with large SCA policies.
notes: Represents: 100 agents x 400 large CIS checks (5+ compliance mappings, long remediation text).
A typical SCA check JSON can be 1-5 KB; this inflates to 16 KB to simulate
compliance-heavy policies (PCI+HIPAA+NIST+CIS+GDPR all in one check).
Total in-flight if WorkersQueue saturates: 100 x 400 x 16 KB = 640 MB RAM peak.
This is the SCA equivalent of heavy_payload_burst.json but uses sca_check payloads.
Manager-side: M1 (WorkersQueue) is the primary bottleneck — each vector<char> is 16 KB.
Agent-side parallel: S1 (m_inMemoryData accumulates 400 x 16 KB = 6.4 MB per agent, but
the REAL risk is 5 policies x 600 checks x 16 KB = 48 MB — unmeasurable from outside).
Compare with sca_burst: same session count, bigger payloads → isolates payload size impact.
